qa_pairs.question: What is a critical formatting detail to remember when adding public keys in a cloudInit YAML script?
qa_pairs.answer: When adding public keys in a cloudInit YAML script, it is crucial to ensure there is a space after the hyphen ("-") before your public key string, as YAML is very particular about whitespace formatting.

qa_pairs.question: What impact does a cloudInit script that specifies users have on the default user of a newly created virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: If a cloudInit script specifies users but does not explicitly include the default user, the script will overwrite the default user that would normally be added. This means the users listed in the cloudInit configuration script will be the only users on the newly created virtual machine.

qa_pairs.question: When using a cloudInit script to define users for a new virtual machine, what is a vital permission consideration?
qa_pairs.answer: It is vital to ensure that at least one of the users defined in the cloudInit script has sudo permissions, as this configuration will make the listed users the only ones on the newly created virtual machine, potentially removing the default user.
